expected_claims: Anthropic announced the Claude 3 model family.
The family includes three models in ascending capability order: Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus.
Opus and Sonnet are available in claude.ai and the Claude API.
The Claude API is generally available in 159 countries.
Haiku will be available soon.
Anthropic believes AI's impact may rival the industrial and scientific revolutions.
Anthropic believes transformative AI could arrive within the coming decade.
Anthropic was founded to focus on AI safety.
notes: Long-form essay, opinion-heavy; entity density is low.

expected_claims: Anthropic released a research paper on interpreting large language models.
The team mapped millions of concepts that activate inside Claude 3 Sonnet.
Golden Gate Claude was a 24-hour research demo.
The demo is no longer available.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet is likely the single best available LLM.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet scores higher than Claude 3 Opus on Anthropic's internal evals.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet is twice the speed of Claude 3 Opus.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet is one-fifth the price of Claude 3 Opus.
Claude 3.5 Sonnet costs $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens.
notes: First claim should be marked is_opinion=true (Simon hedges with 'likely').

expected_claims: Wisdom and intelligence are related but not the same.
Wisdom is roughly 'knowing what to do in a lot of situations'.
Smart people are not necessarily wise.
notes: Essay with very low entity density; the test is whether the model resists inventing entities. Empty list is acceptable and expected.

expected_claims: There is a large gap between understanding how a layer works and reaching state of the art.
Training neural networks has many subtle pitfalls.
A disciplined recipe-like methodology helps avoid common training mistakes.
notes: Author is implied (it's his own blog); accept either Karpathy as a person entity or omitted.

expected_claims: Coroutines declared with async/await syntax are the preferred way to write asyncio applications.
Simply calling a coroutine does not schedule it; it must be awaited or wrapped in asyncio.run().
Tasks are used to schedule coroutines concurrently.
There are three main types of awaitable objects: coroutines, Tasks, and Futures.
notes: Reference docs — expected_action_items should be empty even though the page demonstrates idioms.

expected_claims: Pydantic models inherit from BaseModel and define fields as annotated attributes.
Models are a primary way to define schema in Pydantic.
Models share similarities with Python dataclasses but are designed for validation, serialization, and JSON-schema generation.
A minimal FastAPI app defines an instance and a path operation decorated with @app.get.
Running 'fastapi dev' starts a development server with live reload.
Path operation functions return data that FastAPI serializes to JSON.
notes: Tutorial may produce one or two action items (e.g., 'create main.py and copy the example').

expected_claims: Anthropic is an American AI company headquartered in San Francisco.
Anthropic was founded on January 26, 2021.
Anthropic's founders include former OpenAI members, including the Amodei siblings.
Anthropic develops the Claude series of large language models.
Anthropic focuses on AI safety.
notes: Reference page — expected_action_items must be empty.

expected_claims: Geoffrey Hinton is a British-Canadian computer scientist.
Hinton was born on 6 December 1947.
Hinton is a Nobel Prize laureate.
Hinton is known for his work on artificial neural networks.
notes: May also extract University of Toronto, Google, or other institutions.

expected_claims: A large language model is a neural network trained on a vast amount of text for natural language processing tasks.
The most capable LLMs as of 2026 are based on transformer architectures.
The 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need' introduced transformers.
LLMs can generate, summarize, translate, and analyze text.
RLHF is a technique to align an intelligent agent with human preferences.
RLHF involves training a reward model to represent preferences.
The reward model is then used to train other models through reinforcement learning.
In classical reinforcement learning, an agent learns a policy to maximize reward.
Pydantic does data validation using Python type hints.
Pydantic V2 is a ground-up rewrite with new features and some breaking changes versus V1.
Pydantic Logfire helps monitor applications.
notes: GitHub README — fetch should succeed; expect the SDK install instruction may register as an action item.

expected_claims: The Claude SDK for Python provides access to the Claude API from Python applications.
The SDK requires Python 3.9 or later.
The project is licensed under the MIT License.
notes: Short README — extracted text is only ~77 words, expect a thin result. Good baseline for the JS-rendered-page failure mode.

expected_claims: The paper proposes the Transformer, a sequence transduction architecture based solely on attention mechanisms.
The Transformer dispenses with recurrence and convolutions.
Experiments on machine translation tasks show the Transformer is superior in quality to prior models.
Pre-training plus task-specific fine-tuning achieves substantial gains on NLP benchmarks.
Current NLP systems still require thousands of fine-tuning examples to do well on new tasks.
Humans can perform new language tasks from only a few examples or simple instructions.
The paper investigates few-shot learning with large language models.
notes: Abstract may or may not name GPT-3 directly in the extracted text; entity may be omitted.

expected_claims: Docker provides essential resources for newcomers via the getting-started section.
Docker can optimize development workflows.
notes: Landing page with only ~62 extracted words; entity and claim counts should be small. Reference-style, no real call to action.

expected_claims: 'pnpm create next-app@latest' creates a new Next.js application.
The default setup enables TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, the App Router, and Turbopack.
The default development server runs at http://localhost:3000.
notes: Installation tutorial — explicit calls to action expected ('Create a new app', 'Run the dev server').

expected_claims: AlphaFold is an AI program developed by DeepMind, a subsidiary of Alphabet.
AlphaFold predicts protein structures using deep learning techniques.
AlphaFold 1 placed first at the 13th Critical Assessment of Structure Prediction in December 2018.
